signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 158.9; safe-haven bid competes with 450bp Fed-BoJ carry differential. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD LF net long +41,675 (CFTC, week ending Apr 14), long%=72.0% — extreme positioning. HV30 at 6.5%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 8.2% — options pricing a 1.7 vol-pt premium over realized. AUD/USD at 0.7168, price stalling at 2026 highs — late-trend caution signal. Watch for LF delta turning negative to invalidate.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — 0bp GBP carry differential supports the pair structurally. GBP LF net long +28,426, AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. GBP/USD at 1.3519 mid-range; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Inflation + COT
signals.text: New Zealand Q1 2026 inflation came in above forecasts — NZD/USD at 0.5910. NZD COT LF net short −17,090, long%=21.7% — short positioning. RBNZ rate hike bets picked up — watch 0.6000 as the next level on a sustained move.
